subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Heat Acclimatization
description: A quick, practical guide to Heat Acclimatization.

hook.text: Military recruits arriving for summer training collapse from heat. Two weeks later, they're fine. Why?

buildup.text: The body acclimatizes to heat over 7-14 days of gradual exposure.

discovery.text: Acclimatized bodies sweat sooner, sweat more, and lose less salt per drop—a triple cooling upgrade.

twist.text: Losing acclimatization takes only one week without heat exposure—it's fragile.

climax.text: Most heat casualties occur in the first days of a heat wave—before bodies can adapt.

punchline.text: Your body can learn to handle heat in two weeks. Heat waves won't wait.

quiz.question: How long does heat acclimatization typically take?
